era_context: The Neo-Assyrian Empire under Shalmaneser III continues its military expansion. The Levantine states remain engaged in resistance and coalition-building. In the Eastern Mediterranean, Phoenician maritime trade is flourishing. In Greece, the Archaic period is well underway; in China, the Western Zhou is in its final decades.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's campaigns (general period)
events.description: Shalmaneser III continued his military campaigns during this period as part of his broader strategy of imperial expansion. The Assyrian annals record ongoing military activity, though specific details for individual years are often merged in the surviving records. These campaigns maintained Assyrian pressure on the Levantine states and reinforced Assyrian hegemony in the region.
events.certainty_note: The general pattern is well-attested, but specific year-by-year details are uncertain. Assignment to 846 BCE is approximate.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are reliably documented for 846 BCE as a distinct year.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 846 BCE is minimal. Assyrian royal inscriptions are the only contemporary written sources, and they do not permit precise annual dating. Archaeological evidence is sparse. This year represents a gap in detailed historical documentation.
